nativeRange.habitat: Fast-flowing, highly oxygenated hill streams and rivers with rocky pools, thick riparian vegetation, and pristine water quality. Found in Kerala and Karnataka states.

diet.notes: Tends to accept most high-quality dried foods (flakes, pellets) and benefits from regular offerings of frozen or live foods such as bloodworm, Daphnia, and brine shrimp. Blanched vegetables and spirulina-enriched foods support coloration. Sinking foods are often preferred.

avoidWith: very small fish that may be outcompeted

summary: A large, fast-moving schooling barb from India's Western Ghats known for its bold red and black lateral stripe. Generally considered intermediate care due to cool-water needs and IUCN Endangered status.
careNotes:
IUCN Endangered (EN) — this species has been heavily impacted by wild collection for the aquarium trade. Captive-bred stock from Indian and Thai commercial farms is increasingly available and is strongly preferred over wild-caught. The reclassification from Puntius denisonii to Sahyadria denisonii was published in 2012. Sometimes sold under multiple trade names including 'Roseline Shark' and 'Red Line Torpedo Barb' — these all refer to the same species.

Requires excellent water quality; tends to be intolerant of organic waste accumulation. Weekly water changes of 30–50% are generally advised. A tightly sealed lid is recommended, as this species has been reported to jump. Strong filtration with high water flow (4–5× tank turnover) and good oxygenation reflect its native hill-stream habitat. Uprooting of plants has been documented — robust or rhizome-attached plants are better suited.
breedingNotes: Breeding in home aquariums is considered very difficult and has rarely been achieved without hormone stimulation. Commercial farms in Thailand and India produce captive-bred stock via hormonal induction. Specific triggering conditions are not well-documented for hobbyist settings.

primary.name: SeriouslyFish
primary.notes: Primary source used after Aquarium Co-Op returned 404. SeriouslyFish provided detailed taxonomic, water parameter, and husbandry data. Cross-checked temperature, pH, hardness, tank dimensions, and native range against FishKeepingWorld, FishBase, and AquariumSource.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed scientific name Sahyadria denisonii, max length 15 cm TL, temperature 15–25°C, pH 6.8–7.8, Cyprinidae/Cypriniformes, IUCN Endangered. Consistent with other sources.

additional.name: Fishkeeping World
additional.notes: Confirmed 15 cm adult size, 15–25°C, pH 6.6–7.8, 55 gal min tank, schooling group of 6, omnivore diet, peaceful temperament, lifespan up to 5 years. Used for lifespan lower bound and care level cross-check (rated 'easy' vs. 'intermediate' on other sources — averaged to intermediate).

additional.name: AquariumSource
additional.notes: Confirmed 15 cm, gH 5–25 dGH (aligns with SeriouslyFish 90–447 ppm hardness), 16–25°C, pH 6.6–7.8, escape risk (reported jumping), lifespan up to 5 years. kH from web search aggregate (4–10); combined with user key data (5–15) to yield kH 4–15. Lifespan upper bound 8 years from web search aggregate (5–8 range).

fish.breedingNotes: Rarely bred in captivity without hormone stimulation; commercial production relies on hormonal induction at farms in Thailand and India.
